name: Guerilla In The Midst Part 2
frontText: The tool to open the generator building is to be hidden at one of several pre-agreed locations. The risk of leaks is too great so it must be hidden without anyone seeing, even your own people. Lucrecia will collect the tool later.
backText: Place F G H face-up next to LoS-blocking terrain up to Black from battlefield centre and at least Yellow from each other.

name: Double Locked
frontText: Typical. The safe built into the desk is one of the most secure ever made. Not only near impregnable, it can only be opened by entering a different password into two terminals at once. You reckon one person could do it alone but multiple people would be better.
backText: SET-UP: Add the desk and 2 terminals to the battlefield Yellow from each other and at least Blue from any edge. Use 💻 while ➡️ with terminal:

name: Plague Of Radroaches
frontText: Rachel needs your help exterminating the radroaches. It's one of their many breeding seasons when they are more docile than usual.
backText: SET-UP: Opponent places 3 Radroach Swarms at least Yellow from battlefield edges and each other.

name: Sending A Message
frontText: George Ginger had had enough. "The impact on my farm from the water they use is too great now. I can't make a living. Tried scaring them away, but they didn't take it serious. Make them understand for me?"
backText: To succeed, remove all your opponent's models except one.

name: The Raised Bridge
frontText: The bridge near your settlement had been raised when the control hut was destroyed during fighting decades ago. The old, municipal facility that used to be here held an override. If the circuits can be re-connected, the bridge will lower and a new route to and from your settlement can be opened.
backText: SET-UP: Add 4 heavy consoles at least Blue apart and Blue from any edges.

name: Edens Project
frontText: Eden's jet black deathhawk haircut is easy to recognise. Whilst often mistaken for a Raider by looks, or the Enclave President by name, she actually keeps the various local factions in balance.
backText: Add 2 tokens each representing a Stranger, and place Black from 2 edges. Nominate a model to carry out this Quest.

name: A Legend Returns Part 2
frontText: Alex has heard it wasn't grave robbers but the Brotherhood of Steel.
backText: Place a marker Black from battlefield edge furthest from your deployment. This is a crate. To open the crate, cause 2 damage total - it has 2. When open: Roll a pentagon

name: Getting Technical
frontText: Quinlan says he needs technical documents. He doesn't elaborate why the Brotherhood of Steel need them. Just whether you want the task or not.
backText: During set-up, place 1 additional blank next to each of the first four placed.

name: Grognak The Barbarian #10
frontText: I'm not sure I ever read this issue. '#10 What Sorcery This?'. Gives me some ideas for next time we enter combat. I bet a collector may buy it – once I've perfected Grognak's feat.
backText: As Grognak: #1 Performed above.

name: Sleeper Virus
frontText: "Don't worry, the virus can't harm you. It's targeted only at the Feral Ghouls. You don't have any Feral friends, right?! Needs to spread to be effective though. Seeing as you travel - will you be my vector?" Victor seemed a bit crazy, but then which scientists didn't these days?
backText: Choose one model to start as infected — must start Blue from all friendly models. An infected model infects all models with which they are base-to-base when they perform any action.

name: Adams Menagerie Part 2
frontText: "Do a couple more jobs for me and I'll reward you. The rare Black Bloatfly are about to hatch. Blink and you'll miss it, so grab them before they fly away.
backText: SET-UP: Roll 5

name: Tools Of War Part 1
frontText: It was rumoured to be too good, too efficient, and too unreasonable. Still, someone would probably buy it. The Assaultron prototype had been disassembled and supposedly melted down, but the record in the Robotics Disposal Grounds terminal shows the parts were sent to be dumped elsewhere.
backText: Spread the 3 Assaultron models on the battlefield at least Blue from the edges. These models represent where parts may be. Spend an action to test STR while → with an Assaultron model:

name: Calling All Stations
frontText: For years, Richard Clark tended the small satellite array in this area. Whilst not as large as some arrays, it was still in decent condition. Richard thinks he's found the original signal boosters which could double the array's comms range. They're a bit temperamental and must be switched on in unison to be synchronized.
backText: SET-UP: Place 3 C around one piece of terrain which blocks LoS, at least Black from your deployed models, and at least Green from each other. These are the devices.

name: Capturing Memories
frontText: Turns out that the camera you found recently actually still works. A photo of the team in action would really boost team spirit.
backText: Equip 1 model with Undamaged Camera Item (for free). From start of Round 3, an unengaged model may take a photo with the camera (no action required) when at least half of your starting models (exclude camera user) are all within Yellow of each other and in LoS up to Yellow from the camera user.

name: Giddyup Buttercup Part 1
frontText: "A complete Giddyup Buttercup is one thing, but a working one?! Now *that* is rare. Well, I say working – it snorts, it trots, but it also seems to have gone a bit haywire and has galloped off. Could you track it down and bring it back?"
backText: Place 6 face-up furthest from your models and Yellow from battlefield edge. The token is the Giddyup Buttercup. End of each round: Move it Blue in scatter direction (and follow battlefield edge if needed).

name: Gifts From Above
frontText: The Brotherhood signal lights for their airborne supply drops were easy enough to set-up. They just needed laying out correctly and in a location along one of the day's transport routes. A supply Vertibird should be overhead this area very soon.
backText: SET-UP: Nominate one model in your force to place the signalling lights. Give that model 3 counting tokens.

name: A Bet's A Bet
frontText: The bet was you couldn't take someone on your next outing who would only use a knife and not get killed. It was going to be interesting — for them anyway. . .
backText: Equip one model with just the default improvised weapon. If the model survives the battle and landed at least one attack that caused damage to a non-friendly model, gain 80 caps reward.

name: A Sting In The Tale
frontText: Carefully extracted, Radscorpion venom can remain deadly.
backText: When weapon causes damage, adds 1

name: Grognak The Barbarian #1
frontText: Grognak. Issue 1. Blood on the Harp. I used to love these stories! I wonder if his moves are possible. I could try and imitate them. Maybe that move in which he grabs someone by the feet and throws them against another enemy?
backText: Draw 1 Practiced Boost. Any model may use the Boost. To succeed, use the Boost within Yellow of opposing model. Then, after battle or later, you may sell this comic. Total value based on number of different issues you sell together.

name: Caravan Cheat
frontText: "They cheated me. I swear they were using a concealed extra deck and they stashed it around here near where we played. I can't see it. I've got to know. Can you find it for me?"
backText: SET-UP: Mix 4 Searchables (3 blank and 1 letter) and scatter them Blue from center of the battlefield.

name: The Thief's Trail Part 2A
frontText: Your information leads you to a secluded location. The gas canisters must be here somewhere. Find and retrieve the canisters.
backText: Add a lettered briefcase during set-up which is the gas canisters. You succeed if one of your surviving models is carrying the canisters at the end of the battle.

name: Robotics Engineer
frontText: "Test Synth J287 exploded around here somewhere. There's one part I need a photo of, but it's under development so I can't tell you any details about it. Just take a photo of each piece you find, and hopefully you'll cover it. Be quick though, they'll be of no use if you or anyone else contaminates the scene beforehand."
backText: During Set-up: Scatter 4 Green from center of battlefield. If any model ends an action within Orange and LoS of a token, discard it. If one of your models ends an action within Yellow and LoS of a token, place it on this card.

name: Escort The Witness Part 1
frontText: The Raider ambush that wiped out the Gunner's convoy was brutal and flawless, except for the accidental witness – Lowe, who had been researching mirelurk colonies nearby. The Gunner's boss wants a word with Lowe to discover who needs a 'visit' from the Gunners. You are to escort Lowe to camp.
backText: Deploy Lowe (model or token) with your force.

name: Tactical Analysis
frontText: New tactics and knowledge about others is a valuable commodity in the Wasteland. The quarry workers asked you for combat training only yesterday. Observing this group's tactics could prove a useful source of information.
backText: Each activation, an Elevated model with LoS to an opponent's model may test PER once to study their tactics:

name: USS Constitution Part 2
frontText: "You have our thanks. Accept this in return for your dedication to duty. Prepare to disembark." - Captain Ironsides
backText: Gain Broadsider

name: Revenge Part 1
frontText: The empty mutfruit fields signalled trouble even before you reached your settlement. A rival gang attacked and your brother was killed. Now they will pay.
backText: First, you need names. Test CHA when enemy leader is within range of any of your models. You can only make this test once per battle.

name: Dead Drop
frontText: Your contact has been providing excellent information. You still have no idea who they are but they certainly seem on your side and keep placing information for you in a dead drop hidden amongst these barricades.
backText: SET UP: Place Military Barricades on the battlefield and place 1 caps next to each.

name: Elusive Pet
frontText: "My son's pet mole rat ran off and now he can't catch it in the tunnels it's made. I know it may not be everyone's first choice of pet but they really do have a connection, especially since his mother... well, y'know. Help catch him, will ya?"
backText: SET UP: Place Investigation Markers face-up showing numbers 1-4 on the battlefield, each one Green from two others and at least Black from all edges.

name: Buried Treasure Part 1
frontText: These lands, now covered by a thick layer of rubble, dust and radioactive waste, hide the remnants of a fifty-years old settlement that ran for a long time, welcoming merchants and travellers from all across the Wasteland.
backText: Any of your models can spend an action to Use 🔍 while interacting with any piece of terrain at least Red from all battlefield edges.

name: The Secret Elixir Part 1
frontText: "A Nuka-Cola Corporation field office used to be located around here. I believe a cooler in there has ingredients that could help me figure out how to make my own Nuka-Cola, but some creatures have nested on top of it."
backText: SET-UP: Place a Searchable Black from two battlefield edges — this is the expected location of the cooler.

name: Bounty
frontText: See that one over there? Hard to believe they're a synth, right? There's a reward on their head.
backText: The model that costs the most points on the opposing side is the synth. If you deal the damage that removes the model, gain a 75 caps reward.

name: The Insider
frontText: See that one? That's our contact on the inside. They've been leaking this gang's information to us about another rival group in the area. Try not to kill them. It gives us an edge to exploit.
backText: SET UP: Write down name (and pose if not unique) of an opponent's model. This is the Insider.

name: Adams Menagerie Part 1
frontText: You approach the wagons of a travelling creature show. "When will I remember?! Lock the gate *before* removing the Domestication Unit for repairs! Hey, you! My Deathclaw ran off before I could start controlling it. Don't make that face, it's quite passive. Could you herd it back? The show starts at 6!"
backText: SET-UP: Place Deathclaw in center of battlefield, and on the battlefield edge in your deployment zone.

name: The Collector
frontText: After years poring over reams of paperwork and computer logs across the Commonwealth, Winnifred believes the ultimate collectible – the one unaccounted-for, gold-labelled Nuka-cola Quantum that was part of Nuka-cola's 25-year celebration – was delivered to a machine in this area. It must have got stuck inside.
backText: SET-UP: Include 4 Nuka-cola machines in the terrain and set aside 1 Nuka-cola Quantum item card.

name: Revenge Part 3
frontText: There they are...

name: Nick Valentine Part 3
frontText: A search of the camp in the storm drain reveals a scuffle. The camp was for one person and the gang members' wounds from close-up gunshots — Hal was jumped but got lucky.
backText: Place H face-up by wall nearest battlefield center. A friendly model ending its activation without attacking between Red and Yellow and in LoS of the token may test Cha:

name: A Legend Returns Part 1
frontText: Rock was a legendary Super Mutant Overlord who went on a journey of discovery and found enlightenment. Enlightenment being the name he gave to a big shotgun he gained in the Glowing Sea. It was buried with him but rumors are circulating that it has been seen in the wasteland. You've heard a local may have information about it.
backText: SET-UP: Add marker Red from centre of battlefield. This is a Local. Only you can interact with the local.

name: Karma Incoming
frontText: Oh no. Why them? Here. A few days ago, you were 'borrowing' supplies from a camp when the owner came back. You managed to get away but they swore they'd have their revenge if they ever saw you again. If you can avoid them now – hopefully – they'll forget your face over time.
backText: SET-UP: Have your opponent nominate one of your models (not / / ) – do not explain why.

name: Tools Of War Part 2
frontText: "I know it needs testing before selling it, but really? You think it'll work... like that? You sure we don't need more parts? And I mean a lot more parts."
backText: SET-UP: Include 1 Assaultron model in your force at no cost. Assaultron not usable until ⇨ to begin test: Roll ⬠

name: The Lost Station Part 2
frontText: Now you know the frequency, you can adjust a radio beacon to transmit the radio station locally.
backText: Have your opponent place a radio beacon within Yellow of the centre of the battlefield – they are not told why. After placing, tell your opponent this is a radio beacon and counts as cover.

name: Artificial Insanity Part 2
frontText: Combining the message fragments reveals a hidden entry:
backText: SET UP: Add marker Red from centre of battlefield to represent the Engineer. Only your models can interact with it.

name: Nick Valentine Part 2
frontText: "Not in that gang, huh? Sounds like Hal didn't make it as far as their settlement. There are a lot of storm drains en route here. Maybe he's down one of those? His parents have often had to tell him off for playing there in the past. Heavy rains are coming so, if he is down there, we've got to get him out before they flood."
backText: Have opponent place F G H face-up at least Green from battlefield edges and each other.

name: Revenge Part 2
frontText: The Leader taunts you that you couldn't protect your own brother from a mercenary who they easily raided just a few months ago. You know who he means.
backText: Next, you need to find the mercenary. A local trader's computer may hold their details. Add 🖥️ in building closest to center of battlefield and Use 🖥️ on it. Models with no 🖥️, gain 🖥️ 1 for this test.

name: Pest Removal
frontText: "If it's not one critter eating the crop, it's another damaging the fences. Never ends." Ross will pay you if you help keep the creatures off his farm.
backText: SET UP: Place a token Black from two battlefield edges furthest from your models. This is a creature to be removed.

name: One-Armed Bandit
frontText: The old gambling machine was said to be around here - loaded with caps and broken logic so it's a sure thing. It needs feeding to get it to pay, but it's worth it.
backText: Place a ☐ marker (the slot machine) in a corner of the battlefield Blue from edges. Only you can interact with it.

name: Escort The Witness Part 2A
frontText: The boss's T-51 armour strikes a daunting figure for the untravelled. She is incensed by the impudence of the Raider warlord. She gives you junk worth 92 caps as reward. The Raider warlord that ordered the ambush is less impressed with the service you provided the Gunners, however.
backText: Raider warlord, Tower Tom, has hired you to deliver payback on your opponent. You will gain 20 caps for every opponent model you remove and 30 caps for every  or .

name: The Secret Elixir Part 2
frontText: "Amazing. Well done. This is it. Easy Street with my range of Nuka-Colas." Her excitement is only matched by her disappointment when the cooler is opened revealing only the aged and withered remnants of someone's uneaten lunch.
backText: Have your opponent nominate one of their models. Do not tell them why. This is who sold the information to Harris. She wants them taught a lesson.

name: Makeshift Armor
frontText: The metal in the building debris here could be utilised and turned into armor. Ripping usable pieces off requires a lot of strength, even for Super Mutants.
backText: SET-UP: Place 4 blank Searchables markers (metallic debris) face-up diagonally Blue from each corner of the battlefield.

name: The Lost Watch
frontText: A traveller says they are an armorer and will help you out if you find the pocket watch they lost when they ran for cover during a fierce storm. They would go but the area does not seem safe.
backText: During set-up, add a token with a letter to the pool.

name: Misplaced Secrets
frontText: The Brotherhood messenger went missing around here. The recovery team had given up trying to find the tactical survey notes the messenger was carrying. Now they've left, maybe you can find them? Could contain some useful insights.
backText: SET UP: If not already present, add these Green from the center and at least Yellow from each other:

name: Potential Witness
frontText: "I swear I saw some synths by the downtown church yesterday. No, I didn't think they were real either. I think they saw me too so I just ran and didn't look back. What if they're after me...?"
backText: Have opponent nominate one of your models without knowing why. Start of each round: Scatter 1 Battered Gen 1 Synth Blue from selected model. End of round: Synth shoots once with Institute Laser Pistol at nominated model, then remove Synth.

name: The Missing Trooper Part 1
frontText: The Enclave Outpost agreed to let you go if you searched for Fabrizi, a Hellfire Trooper missing since yesterday. Mid-30s, though looks 50.
backText: SET-UP: Place Yellow from battlefield center.

name: The Armor Fanatic Part 1
frontText: Rumor has it that a guy that used to live in this area a long time ago was obsessed with everything power armor, but had disappeared one day. He said he had treasure in his safe. Maybe it was nothing, but worth checking.
backText: Place a token on the building nearest the centre of the battlefield in an elevated position – this is a safe.

name: Treasure Hunt
frontText: The map shows a grid overlaid on a sketch of the area and a single X in grid squares B2. Not much of a treasure map, but it was a start. Digging at the first X uncovered a small box containing some caps and the grid co-ordinates of another X - the location of the next treasure.
backText: Place a Searchable Black from two battlefield edges – this is the X. Use at +2 skill while with the X:

name: USS Constitution Part 1
frontText: "You visit this fine vessel in trying times. We appear to be running low on cannonballs. While we fix our mobility issues, how about you fetch some ammunition for us? There are stocks nearby." — Captain Ironsides
backText: Place a Green from two battlefield edges.

name: Food Hunt
frontText: Meat and plenty of it. The Super Mutant diet is simple but requires gathering lots of meat.
backText: Assign this Quest to a specific model in your force. Each time it removes a non-robot model with close combat, test STR:

name: The Armor Fanatic Part 2
frontText: The safe is full of various official paperwork about the T-45 armor. Seems worthless to anyone but a fan. One document does catch your eye — a report on the first manufacturing runs.
backText: SET-UP: Add marker Green from centre of battlefield. This is a Stranger. Either player can interact with Stranger as normal (inform your opponent).

name: Enemy Of My Enemy
frontText: "You don't need to know my name, but we have a common foe. You give me information about their weaknesses, and I'll see you right. What do you say?"
backText: During the battle, each time one of your unengaged models is within Yellow of your opponent's Leader and that Leader rolls an X, you may choose to place 1 cap on this card. After the battle, gain 50 caps per counter, then roll pentagon: If equal or more than the number of counters, discard the counters and this quest can be attempted again.

name: The Crimson Caravan
frontText: "One of our caravans forgot to pick-up a crate. It needs to remain secret too so no unauthorised personnel can go near it or the recipient won't want it."
backText: SET-UP: Pick one corner of the battlefield and place a Black from two battlefield edges.

name: The Thief's Trail Part 1
frontText: The gas canisters were there last night, and now nothing but faint tracks leading towards the hills. Gaining on the thief, the tracks start to fade – you need a visual on the culprit.
backText: If one of your models ends any action during the battle at the highest reachable point of the terrain on the battlefield, they can see where the thief is headed - Go to Port 2a.

name: Nick Valentine Part 1
frontText: In the dim light of Nick's office, he tells you the details of the case. "Hal's parents were delayed returning from selling their livestock at the Brahmin market due to a rad storm. After recent local Raider activity, Hal headed north to find them but hasn't been seen since. They're worried he's been coerced into a gang out that way. Could you check if he's among them?"
backText: Nominate 1 model to complete this Quest: At the end of each action, add a caps to this card for each opposing model within Yellow and LoS. Each opposing model can only be used once to gain a token during a battle.

name: Cease Fire
frontText: Most of the independent turrets left by the Brotherhood in this area have been disabled. The recent flooding has corroded the combat inhibitors of the remaining ones making them erratic as they seem to ignore some groups, and not others (especially you). The locals just can't get near this one. Could you do remove it for them?
backText: SET-UP: Have opponent add a caps at least Green from battlefield edges and Orange from terrain – do not tell them why until placed. This is a Machinegun Turret Mk III. It will only attack your models – do not tell opponent that. Give it 1 fewer eye than usual. A successful Use will disable it.

name: The Missing Trooper Part 3
frontText: A broken wagon. Slaughtered Brahmin. The body of a farmer. It's a mess. Surely the work of Super Mutants. No Fabrizi though. Nearby are some residential ruins – the houses blown from existence long ago but their cellars still intact – the signs of a Super Mutant camp are clear. You hear yelling from the cellars. Is it Fabrizi or are there other captives?
backText: SET-UP: Scatter 3 blank and 1 numbered Investigation Marker Blue from battlefield center:

name: Horner Herder
frontText: "Those dumb Bighorners. Always coming back to eat my crops. I need them to eat the weeds around the area, not my crops! I tried shouting at them but, well, that's how I got the broken arm. They need a proper scare. Do me a favour and shoo them away from my seedlings, will you?"
backText: SET-UP: Place 5 scattered Green from center of battlefield. Each is a Bighorner.

name: Weapons Engineer
frontText: "I've tried calibrating this rifle but I'm not sure it works in the field. Could you try it out and adjust it? It should be more accurate than the regular rifle."
backText: Select 1 model with an Institute Laser Rifle. When using the weapon, roll 1 fewer. Each time you hit an opposing model (regardless of damage), add to this card.

name: The Missing Trooper Part 2
frontText: The drops of blood cease. The trail appears to have gone cold but your search near the final drops reveals Brahmin prints — two sets, side-by-side. And there — faint wheel tracks and several squashed Mutfruit. Probably a farmer with a wagon. Injured, Fabrizi must have grabbed a ride with them.
backText: When one of your models is engaged with an opposing model, it may make a PER Test. (Each opposing model may only be tested once during a battle.)

name: Radiation Ideation
frontText: The farmer raved about the uses of radioactive waste in crop farming – it could be develop better crops, light the barn at night, or keep you warm – what couldn't it do? Maybe Children of Atom had swayed the farmer's mind. He needs more waste though. Turning down his offer of crops as compensation, he says he has some information you'll find useful if you'll help.
backText: SET-UP: Add 3 radioactive containers Green from battlefield centre. These do not emit radiation.

name: The Stand In
frontText: "I injured my leg and now can't meet my contact. It's important. Could you meet them for me? Just take care of what they need. Tell 'em Blanco sent you."
backText: SET UP: Add marker Green from center of battlefield. This is a Stranger with which only your models can interact (inform your opponent).

name: Know Your Enemy
frontText: Occasional pieces of information about an enemy are good, but true insight is often derived from combining data. The group you are soon to face has been active in this area for some time. Scrape together whatever information you can find on terminals in the area and you may be able to glean some data that can give you an advantage against them.
backText: Scatter 2 [Computer icon] Blue from battlefield center.

name: Echoes Of Yesterday
frontText: The sound of the TV had started a few days ago. 'Tomb of Amun-Ra' - great movie - but not on a loop and definitely not with its reverb travelling across the still air at 4am, making sleep nigh impossible. Given that you'll be nearby, you might as well turn it off.
backText: Have your opponent place a counter on the battlefield to represent the TV - do not tell them what it is until placed.

name: Protectron Strike Team
frontText: The promo video was old but the Protectrons were unused.
backText: Include 2 Protectron models in your force with Hand Lasers. Judged individually, each model a Protectron removes gives it Per +1 (which accumulates). If either/both survive the battle, use this Quest again to field the remaining Protectrons with same Per bonus and ❤ when they finished their previous battle. They can not be repaired.

name: Grognak The Barbarian #3
frontText: I remember this issue. There's this move from 'Jungle of the Bat-Babies' in which Grognak does this special twist that outwits one of the main antagonists. It'd be awesome to see that on the battlefield!
backText: As Grognak: #1 Performed above.

name: Artificial Insanity Part 1
frontText: Hacking the old computer records at the train yard for leads on supplies was easy enough, but the last entry stood out. Too quirky to be by the workers, and too recent to be part of the old records. Steed. It had to be Steed. The only hacker in the region with the ability to insert such messages. The message is incomplete and there are directions to find the other parts.
backText: SET UP: Add 2 [computer icon] Blue diagonally from opposite corners of the battlefield.

name: Handy Delivery Part 2
frontText: The buyer Ernest told you about had been surprisingly hard to find - after all, how many Mr Handys can there be around here?
backText: Add face-up during set-up within Yellow of battlefield center. This is the buyer. Only you can interact with him.

name: Radio Interference
frontText: Someone keeps placing radio jammers in the area camouflaged as all sorts of ordinary objects. Carpenter asks you to destroy these as they're playing havoc with listening to the few stations around. You can sell them for parts.
backText: SET UP: Have your opponent place 2 caps on the battlefield at least Blue from any edge and each other. These are potential radio jammers. Any damage removes a token.

name: Agent Of Secrets
frontText: She doesn't give her name. There are some documents in a car which must be retrieved and then the car destroyed. Were these documents from when the car was abandoned or placed there recently?
backText: SET-UP: Include at least one car in the battlefield terrain.

name: Escort The Witness Part 2B
frontText: With Lowe out of the picture, the Gunner's boss demands you find out what happened. You need to examine the location where the attack took place and figure out who was responsible.
backText: During set-up of scenario not using [blue cap icon], mix and evenly distribute [blue cap icon] showing A) B) [blank] [blank] [blank]

name: Problems In Paradise
frontText: Why someone would hack the Eyebot to take compromising pictures of Davenport was another story, and one he wasn't telling. Davenport wants you to find the Eyebot and wipe the images. The Eyebot must not be destroyed or they'll know he's onto them - just delete them.
backText: SET-UP: After deployment, place token (or model) to represent the Eyebot, at least Black from all other models.

name: Robotic Secrets
frontText: "Most Sentry Bot processor chips get destroyed by the robot's self-destruct, but I know where you could extract one for me." Whatever Anna wants the chip for, you know it's best not to ask and even better not to know. Finding the intact but defunct Sentry Bot was one thing, extracting its chip is another.
backText: SET-UP: Place a Sentry Bot model Yellow from battlefield centre.

name: Battle Codex Part 3
frontText: "The Battle Tactics section of the Codex needs information on the bigger picture. Observe how aggressors interact." - Beth
backText: Per +1

name: Tags Of The Fallen
frontText: Both NCR and Caesar's Legion have asked you to give them any NCR Dog Tags you find.
backText: SET-UP: Have opponent place 4 caps up to Black from center and at least Red from each other each representing a Dog Tag.

name: Guerilla In The Midst Part 1
frontText: If anyone will fight the good fight, it is Lucrecia. Too many have gone missing already, so she works tirelessly to prevent more. Some people are being held captive in a generator building. She needs a special tool which can be used to open the loading bay door – most people mistake it for a piece of junk.
backText: Pickpocket the opposing Leader to gain the tool you need. After an action when engaged with the opposing Leader, make an Opposed Skill Test: Your Agi v their PER. No action is required. Add ⬠ if Leader is outnumbered.

name: Adams Menagerie Part 3
frontText: "You're going where? Scorchbeasts used to roam here. Maybe I could clone one. Imagine the crowds that would come!"
backText: Set-up: Place 4 blank Searchables - each Black from a different corner. These are Scorchbeast bones. ⇄ to pick-up.

name: Recipes Of The Wasteland
frontText: Some old Wasteland recipes require strange ingredients. One local has asked you to find the nest of some creatures.
backText: Add face-up during set-up within Yellow of battlefield center. Draw a Creature. Only you can interact with this. After resolving Creature, choose whether to add to grab a specimen, then:

name: The Lost Station Part 1
frontText: Ex-Vertibird pilot – Joe – describes an amazing music radio station he used to receive when flying over the Wasteland. Rumors say it travels the Wasteland hacking into radio beacons along the way to transmit the signal. Joe misses it. Would you find a way so it can be received here?
backText: When elevated, a model can spend an action to test PER:

name: The Thief's Trail Part 2B
frontText: Without seeing the perpetrator, the trail goes cold. Asking around about anyone suddenly with gas to sell is probably the only way to find them now.
backText: Add face-up during set-up within Yellow of battlefield center. Draw a Stranger. Only you can interact with this. Successfully interact with the Stranger: Go to Part 2a.

name: Wounded Glowing One
frontText: Settlement Leader Lever needs help. A Bloated Glowing One escaped the cull. Almost dead, it was seen limping off his way somewhere. If it reaches a source of radiation, it'll heal and could strike again. Find it by following the trail of its blood and kill it. Lever has some local knowledge for you if you destroy it.
backText: SET UP: Place Yellow from the battlefield center.

name: Junk Collection
frontText: Junk is strewn across the Wasteland and all can be exchanged for caps. Over the years, the easy pickings have gone and it requires some digging through the rubble. Still, caps are caps no matter the source.
backText: SET-UPS: Place 6 counting tokens on the battlefield at least Black apart and Green from edges.

name: Battle Codex Part 2
frontText: The Codex's Threat Identification Guide needs accurate behavioural intelligence from the field. Already impressed by your observations for the Codex, Senior Scribe Beth tasks you with creating annotated illustrations of some of the Wasteland's inhabitants.
backText: To observe enough detail for an accurate report, one of your unengaged models must spend 1 action within Green of an enemy model - This action counts as a Move but the model does not move. Each action can only observe 1 model.

name: Handy Delivery Part 1
frontText: Mr Handy parts have become hard to find recently. Local trader Ernest Watson wants you to pick some up from his brother, Alfred, and then deliver them to one of Ernest's customers.
backText: Add face-up during set-up within Yellow of battlefield center. This is Alfred. Only you can interact with him.

name: Tales Of The Lurker
frontText: Rumors are spreading that your settlement is haunted. People swear they are being followed by a presence and some have even been injured too. It's being called 'The Lurker'.
backText: SET UP: Add marker to battlefield edge nearest enemy Leader.

name: Giddyup Buttercup Part 2
frontText: The Giddyup Buttercup has formed an attachment to your Leader – following them everywhere, sleeping at their feet. If you didn't know better, you'd swear it had feelings.
backText: SET-UP: Place G in contact with your Leader.

name: The Poisoned Deathclaw
frontText: The trappers were now uncertain how much poison they had put in the Brahmin carcass used to lure the Deathclaw. They didn't want to use too much in case it tainted the meat (and their profit), but had they given it too little? Rather than risk facing an angry (and unsubdued) Deathclaw, they ask if you would check on it for them, and finish it off if required?
backText: SET-UP: Place a model or token representing the Deathclaw Blue from center of battlefield.

name: The Time Lock Safe
frontText: "Seriously. The old guy told me that's how some of the small safes worked at Lexington Bank back then. They can be opened at a specific time just once a day. And that beeping sound people hear around there? That's it opening. How should I know when?! All I know is roughly where it is. Just give me my share if you get to it."
backText: The safe only opens at one moment each day.

name: Artificial Insanity Part 3
frontText: SET UP: Have opponent place up to Black from the battlefield centre. This is the air vent. Assign the special EM grenade to one model. Model must use to hit air vent. If grenade scatters, model can pick grenade up from where it landed. Model may trigger grenade on its next activation after the successful Triggering the grenade fries the A.I. Seed sends you a reward.

name: Photo Shoot
frontText: "I need a subject for the cover of my magazine. Can I take a photo of you in combat? Needs to be something dramatic. Only problem is the clouds are making the light unpredictable."
backText: Nominate one of your models as the photo's subject. Add Consequence to Event deck after first Event is resolved.

name: The Missing Sample
frontText: William's field trip had been a success – right up until he discovered the pheromone sample he had collected was no longer in his bag.
backText: SET-UP: Mix 4 Investigation markers (3 blank and 1 letter) and add diagonally Blue from each corner of the battlefield. Pick-up the token with the letter (the sample) during the battle.

name: Battle Codex Part 1
frontText: The Brotherhood is compiling a Codex to assist in developing our skills through knowledge about the Wasteland and its inhabitants.
backText: Select 1 Field Scribe in your force to perform this Quest.

name: Buried Treasure Part 2
frontText: As you wipe off the dust, you realise this is more than just junk. A palm-sized family portrait, curiously ornamented, rests in your hands. You scour the faces in the picture until you stop to look at the eyes of a little kid who looks very familiar. Could it be. . .
backText: During set-up, assign the portrait to one of your models.

name: Innocent Bystander
frontText: *screaming* NOOO! PLEASE, NOOO! DON'T KILL ME! NOOO! HELP!
backText: SET-UP: Deploy model (Bystander) within Yellow of one of your models.

name: Target Practice
frontText: This used to be a combat training ground for some of the Brotherhood. The pop-up targets still work. Jumped out of my skin first time I went through here. Those mannequins are almost as creepy as the real things.
backText: During set-up, spread 6 Feral Ghoul models across the battlefield. These are mannequins. Start of each round, flip 🍀 for each mannequin. 🍀 means stand the mannequin up; otherwise, lay it down.

name: The Stolen Key
frontText: The limping settler approaches you.
backText: Have opponent place token at least Black from all battlefield edges without knowing why. The creature was last seen here.

name: Deathclaw Statues Part 1
frontText: The Deathclaw statue had always been a weird choice. Why here? Had this been a kids' park? If so, why such a realistic Deathclaw? No matter though. You're here for the secret compartment you saw in that book. It's one of the four statues near here. Which one though?
backText: SET-UP: Place the Deathclaw model Blue from centre of battlefield. Once per battle, you can ⇄ Use 🔍:

name: Grognak The Barbarian #2
frontText: The second issue 'Cometh the Trickster' is a classic. Amazing battle moves. I reckon I could use that. Maybe not the somersault though.
backText: As Grognak: #1 Performed above.

name: The Armor Fanatic Part 3
frontText: "Could be that underground storage facility where that feral ghoul nest got out of control. You know it? Group of settlers emptied a tanker of fuel in and set it alight. The stench!"

name: Boomer Bomber
frontText: "The others say the bomber's nowhere near here, but I just have a feeling... Call it a Boomer's hunch. Could you help me and have a look out from these areas for me? If it is there, you won't be able to miss it."
backText: If by the end of the battle, your models (combined) have been within Red of each battlefield corner and the center at any time: Add the Consequence next battle.

name: Big T's Task
frontText: Even for a Super Mutant, Big T is tall and powerful. He's also leader of the Super Mutants in this region and is willing to allow your passage through the area — if you complete a job for him.... He wants information on the significant members of local groups, but he demands a constant supply or the deal's off.
backText: When one of your models is within Orange of a Unique opposing model, Test Int. If successful, gain caps.

name: Blessing Of Atom
frontText: Blessing the bodies of the fallen in Atom's name — giving them spiritual strength whether dead or just incapacitated. Spread the love of Atom and take a little for your trouble too. Who could have an issue with that for such a valuable service?
backText: During set-up, specify which model can give blessings. When any non-creature model is removed, place a token in its place to mark the body.

name: Lost Plans
frontText: Few know the Vertibird that crashed carried important orders for the Brotherhood's next moves in the area. That holotape is likely to be valuable to the Brotherhood, or maybe others may want it more.
backText: SET UP: Add all crashed Vertibird pieces to the battlefield, at least Red from each other and battlefield edges.

name: Potential Partnership
frontText: One of your group says they are meeting a contact later who is a major clothing trader. Could be great for your settlement.
backText: SET-UP: Secretly select 1 model in your force which is not a , , or

name: Deathclaw Statues Part 2
frontText: The book is a study of Deathclaws which holds them in a reverence that is disturbing. Signing themselves as 'Cult of Claw', the writer's final, hastily written entry describes the cult being raided — asking the book's finder to free the young one in the secret den by releasing the chain above its pit.
backText: Gain 112 caps hidden with chain. Tell opponent a roar comes from below the battlefield's centre. At end of round, place Deathclaw Orange (random direction) from battlefield centre as it bursts out of a concealed pit.

name: Wasteland Health Survey
frontText: Doctor Powers is gathering blood samples from different parts of the Wasteland to study. She believes a cure could be found for common ailments, as well as giving her a possibility to adapt known drugs to work with other species.
backText: Assign this Quest to a specific model in your force. Each time it deals damage in Close Combat to an opposing model it has not damaged already, place a on this card.

name: House Rules Part 1
frontText: Mr. House requests you perform a task for him.
backText: Have opponent select one of their models (not their Leader, or a Creature, Robot or Dog). Do not explain why.

name: House Rules Part 2
frontText: "It wasn't me, I swear. A Securitron told me to leave, so I did. What's that? What did it look like? Actually, it did have a weird antenna, and I could have sworn it sounded like that NCR Engineer, Bellamy – must have been the alcohol. Always tinkering in his shack that one. Think he's clever - everyone knows you can unlock his shack using terminals if you know the trick."
backText: SET-UP: Place 2 [computer]. One scattered Blue from Battlefield center and the other scattered Red from Battlefield center.

name: House Rules Part 3
frontText: Inside the shack is a workbench covered by a tarpaulin. Underneath you find a schematic of a radio-control device with microphone and another of a Securitron's head with an unusual looking antenna drawn on. Bellamy's been overriding the robot using remote control, and raided the gambler's slot machine after telling them to leave. This compromised robot needs deactivating.
backText: SET-UP: Arm one of your models with a Pulse Gun. Place a Securitron Mk I at Battlefield center which immediately shoots any model that attacks it using 3 and the Securitron SMG.

name: A Legend Returns Part 3
frontText: The crate does not contain Rock's shotgun but his bladed helmet instead. Make the legend's presence felt once more.
backText: Super Mutants only:
